fields.name: Dallas to New York LTL
fields.seo-title: Dallas, TX to New York, NY LTL Freight | WARP
fields.seo-meta-description: Ship LTL freight from Dallas to New York: 1,617-mile lane, 5–7 day transit, 10 carriers. Get instant rates and real-time tracking through WARP.
fields.hero-headline: Dallas, TX to New York, NY LTL Freight
fields.subheadline: Long-haul LTL across 1,617 miles with 5–7 day transit. 10 vetted carriers, optimized linehaul routing, and real-time exception alerts from pickup to delivery.
fields.body-content:
The Dallas, TX to New York, NY LTL lane is a 1,617-mile long-haul corridor connecting the South Central and Northeast regions. Long-haul LTL at this distance typically routes through multiple terminals, making carrier selection and transit predictability critical. WARP operates this corridor with 10 vetted carriers, targeting 5–7 business day transit through optimized linehaul routing.

WARP's LTL service on this lane includes pallet-level tracking from pickup through delivery, managed appointment scheduling, and delivery confirmation handled by WARP's operations team. Every shipment receives real-time scan events at pickup, each in-transit checkpoint, and final delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change — delayed pickups, missed scans, or appointment changes are flagged and escalated before they become problems. Carrier coordination, rescheduling, and resolution happen without requiring shipper follow-up.

Cross-dock facilities at Dallas Mega Hub, Houston Terminal, Memphis Freight Hub support consolidation and re-routing on this corridor. When exceptions occur — weather delays, equipment failures, or volume surges — this infrastructure enables alternate routing without starting the shipment process over. Hurricane season Jun-Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct-Dec.

LTL rates on the Dallas to New York lane typically range from $5,348 to $7,131 depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and accessorials. Key cost drivers include NMFC classification, shipment density, and services like liftgate or inside delivery. At 1,617 miles, the per-mile linehaul component averages approximately $3.86/mile. Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item. These are modeled estimates — enter your shipment details at wearewarp.com/quote for a real-time, all-in rate.

This lane is a strong fit for 1–6 pallet shipments with standard or appointment-based delivery requirements, recurring retail or distribution replenishment freight, and any LTL load where real-time tracking and proactive exception management matter more than finding the lowest one-time rate. Shippers consolidating carrier relationships to reduce vendor complexity see the most value on corridors like Dallas to New York.

Get an instant LTL rate for the Dallas to New York lane at wearewarp.com/quote. Compare carrier options, book with a single click, and track every shipment with real-time milestone updates. No commitment required. See detailed operating data, pricing factors, and frequently asked questions for this corridor below.
fields.traditional-ltl:
Quote Speed: 2–24 hours via phone/email
Pallet Tracking: No pallet-level visibility
Appointment Scheduling: Manual phone coordination
Carrier Comparison: Manual spreadsheets
Booking: Email chains, 30–60 min
Consolidation: Limited to single carrier network
Exception Handling: Reactive, hours to discover
Freight Class Pricing: Opaque, accessorials added after quote
Damage Risk: Limited visibility until claim filed
fields.warp-ltl:
Quote Speed: Under 2 minutes, self-serve
Pallet Tracking: Real-time scan events at every milestone
Appointment Scheduling: Managed by WARP operations team
Carrier Comparison: Side-by-side dashboard with performance data
Booking: One-click from quote to BOL
Consolidation: Cross-dock consolidation with flexible routing
Exception Handling: Proactive alerts within 30 minutes
Freight Class Pricing: All-in rates with NMFC and accessorial breakdown
Damage Risk: Photo documentation at pickup with proactive alerts

Why the Dallas to New York Corridor Matters

The Dallas to New York LTL lane is a freight corridor connecting the South Central and Northeast regions. At 1,617 miles, this long-haul corridor typically routes through multiple terminals, making carrier selection and transit planning critical. Standard LTL transit runs 5–7 business days. WARP operates this lane with 10 vetted carriers and common equipment including Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Cross-dock facilities at Dallas Mega Hub, Houston Terminal, Memphis Freight Hub support consolidation and transit optimization for this corridor. Hurricane season Jun-Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct-Dec.

  • ▸ 1,617-mile corridor connecting South Central and Northeast regions
  • ▸ 5–7 business day standard transit for LTL service
  • ▸ 10 vetted carriers with Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled) capacity
  • ▸ Cross-dock access at Dallas Mega Hub, Houston Terminal, Memphis Freight Hub
  • ▸ Hurricane season Jun-Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct-Dec.

LTL Pricing Factors: Dallas to New York

LTL rates on the Dallas to New York lane typically range from $5,348 to $7,131, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand. Key cost drivers include NMFC freight classification, shipment density, and accessorials such as liftgate, inside delivery, or limited-access pickup. At 1,617 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $3.86/mile). Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices. These are modeled estimates. Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

Best-Fit Shipments: Dallas to New York LTL

WARP is a strong fit for LTL shipments on the Dallas to New York lane when you need predictable transit, real-time visibility, and operational support without managing carrier relationships directly.

  • ▸ 1–6 pallet shipments with standard or appointment-based delivery requirements
  • ▸ Recurring retail or distribution replenishment freight on this corridor
  • ▸ Shipments requiring real-time tracking and proactive exception management
  • ▸ Freight that needs transparent, all-in pricing without manual quote follow-up
  • ▸ Shippers consolidating carrier relationships to reduce vendor complexity
  • ▸ Freight benefiting from cross-dock consolidation at Dallas Mega Hub or Houston Terminal

Cross-Dock Infrastructure

Cross-dock facilities at Dallas Mega Hub and Houston Terminal support consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur on this corridor. This infrastructure enables better transit reliability on the Dallas to New York lane.

Transparent Pricing

All-in LTL rates with no hidden fees. Fuel surcharges, accessorials, and linehaul are broken out clearly. See exactly what you're paying before you book.

Frequently Asked Questions: Dallas to New York LTL

How much does LTL freight from Dallas, TX to New York, NY cost?

Estimated LTL rates on this lane range from $5,348 to $7,131, based on freight class, weight, pallet count, and current market conditions. Enter your shipment details on WARP for an instant, all-in rate.

What is the transit time for LTL shipments from Dallas to New York?

Standard LTL transit on the Dallas to New York lane is 5–7 business days. This 1,617-mile corridor's actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Dallas to New York lane?

WARP operates this lane with approximately 10 vetted carriers. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and on-time performance history.

Can I track my LTL shipment from Dallas to New York in real time?

Yes. Every WARP shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly without requiring shipper follow-up.

What equipment is available for LTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Dallas to New York corridor includes Dry Van, Reefer (temp-controlled). Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.

What affects LTL freight rates from Dallas to New York?

Key rate factors on this lane include freight class (NMFC classification), shipment weight and density, pallet count, accessorial services (liftgate, inside delivery, limited access), seasonal demand, and current carrier capacity. Fuel surcharges are applied separately based on current diesel prices.

What makes WARP different from traditional LTL freight brokers?

WARP provides self-serve instant quoting (under 2 minutes vs. hours with traditional brokers), a side-by-side carrier comparison dashboard, one-click booking with digital BOL, real-time tracking with proactive exception alerts, and managed operations where WARP handles carrier coordination and resolution directly.

Ready to Ship LTL Freight from Dallas to New York?
